items.title: Exsim Hospitality unit secures RM63.5mil interior design works contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Exsim Hospitality Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Exsim Concepto Sdn Bhd (ECSB), has accepted a letter of award from Cosmopolitan Avenue Sdn Bhd for the design and build of interior works under a RM63.5 million contract. In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the hospitality company said the works commence on July 9, 2026 and are scheduled for completion on Dec 31, 2026, with a defects liability period of 12 months commencing from the date of Certificate of Practical Completion of the completed works. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia must adapt to new security threats
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia can no longer rely on conventional approaches to national security as emerging threats now extend beyond the military, police and enforcement agencies, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the old understanding of security as the sole responsibility of security forces was no longer sufficient, as threats, particularly in cybersecurity, now reached every home, individual and student. "The old understanding was that security was the responsibility of the security forces, the military, police and enforcement agencies.

items.title: Aizo Group secures RM9mil road and drainage project from Xense Design
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aizo Group Bhd's subsidiary Aizo Construction Sdn Bhd has secured a RM9.09 million subcontract for road and drainage works in Cheras, Selangor. The contract was awarded by Xense Design Sdn Bhd on behalf of Esan Juta Sdn Bhd for infrastructure works supporting a proposed transit-oriented development (TOD). Executive director Ahmad Rahizal Ahmad Rasidi said the contract reflects continued confidence in Aizo's capability to undertake infrastructure-related works.

items.title: Johor polls: BN's disciplined campaign gave it an advantage, says analyst
items.summary: Its executive director, Hisommudin Bakar, said BN successfully anchored its campaign on the performance of caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, whose popularity and administration became the coalition's biggest electoral asset. Instead, he said, bread-and-butter issues remained the dominant concerns throughout the campaign. In contrast, Hisommudin said PH struggled to establish a defining campaign theme capable of capturing voters' attention.

items.title: Alpha IVF raises stake in Singapore's unit for RM3.57mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alpha IVF Group Bhd has raised its stake in Alpha International Women's Specialists (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. (AIWSS) to 96.53 per cent after subscribing for additional shares worth S$1.15 million (RM3.57 million) through a rights issue. AIWSS offered 150 million new shares to its existing shareholders via a rights issue on June 12, 2026.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 9, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continued to dampen investor sentiment. In the crypto market, Bitcoin climbed to RM256,876. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia must move beyond race, religion debates amid new security threats
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed concern over the growing use of state, racial and religious sentiments, warning that such divisions could threaten national harmony and security if left unchecked. That is what concerns me,” he said when officiating the National Security Month 2026 programme organised by the National Security Council (MKN) today. Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and National Security director-general Datuk Raja Nurshirwan Zainal Abidin.

items.title: Deputy minister: 81pc of Klang Valley workers report improved job performance under flexible work arrangements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — A study on the impact of the flexible work arrangements policy by the Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma) has found that 81 per cent of workers in the Klang Valley reported improved job performance, clear evidence that work flexibility drives productivity. In Johor, he said 77 per cent of workers were more productive when given the freedom to set their own start and end times, while 64.4 per cent said working from home made their jobs easier. In Penang, 77 per cent of employers reported a noticeable boost in operational efficiency after adopting the flexible work arrangements.

items.title: Hartanah Kenyalang bags RM283.9mil project from JKR Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hartanah Kenyalang Bhd's unit Hartanah Construction Sdn Bhd has secured a RM283.9 million contract from the Public Works Department (JKR) Sarawak to build Wisma JKR Sarawak in Kuching. In a bourse filing, the property developer and construction company said the project is the largest contract secured by Hartanah Construction to date. Hartanah said the contract is expected to contribute positively to the company's earnings and net assets for the financial year ending Oct 31, 2026, and throughout the construction period.

items.title: Iraq will not leave Opec but seeks fair output quota, PM says
items.summary: BAGHDAD: Iraq will not leave Opec, but is seeking a fair output quota within the oil producer group, Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi said in an interview with Saudi-owned Al Arabiya TV, according to his office. Reuters reported in an exclusive in June that Iraq, the group's second-largest producer after Saudi Arabia and one of its five founding members, had considered leaving Opec if the group did not allow Baghdad to significantly increase oil production. Meanwhile, the United Arab Emirates raised its crude output to near record highs above 3.8 million barrels per day in June after it quit OPEC to escape production caps, two people familiar with production data said on Monday.

items.title: Indonesia rolls out B50, moves to end diesel imports
items.summary: Energy and Mineral Resources Minister, Bahlil Lahadalia, said Indonesia consumes between 38 million and 40 million kilolitres of diesel annually and previously imported around three million to four million kilolitres a year before the introduction of B50. He said the programme would increase domestic crude palm oil (CPO) demand from 15.2 million tonnes to between 16.3 million and 17 million tonnes annually, helping provide greater market certainty for the country's oil palm farmers. He said B50 would also increase greenhouse gas emissions reductions from 39.66 million tonnes of carbon dioxide to around 44.46 million tonnes.

items.title: Johor polls: Bersatu denies 120 Pulai members have switched support to PH
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pulai Bersatu has denied claims that more than 120 of its members and leaders have declared support for Pakatan Harapan, describing the allegation as political propaganda ahead of the Johor polling day. Pulai Bersatu acting division chief Che Wan Farid Che Wan Abd Rahman said those who made the declaration were no longer party members and some had left Bersatu, contested as independent candidates or joined other political parties. Che Wan Farid said the allegation was a negative tactic that could affect Bersatu's image, adding that claims the party had neglected its members were unfounded.

items.title: Anwar raises concern over racial issues in Johor polls
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned against Malaysia remaining trapped in old racial and regional narratives, saying such polemics were worrying as Johor heads to the polls. Without naming any party, Anwar said some issues being raised in Johor involving sentiments over districts and states were worrying. "Sometimes old issues, such as race… look at the polemics (being played up) in Johor now, where sentiments over districts and states have become big issues that threaten the nation.

items.title: PTT Synergy to sell land in Klang for RM17mil to improve finances
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PTT Synergy Group Bhd plans to sell a freehold industrial property in Bandar Bukit Raja, Klang, for RM17 million to cut costs and improve its financial position. The property will be disposed of by the company's indirect wholly owned subsidiary PTT Assets Sdn Bhd to WDG Resources Sdn Bhd under a sale and purchase agreement. The company expects to record a gain of RM2 million from the transaction, based on the property's carrying value of RM15 million as at June 30, 2025.

items.title: Police sergeant begins 14-month jail term after Court of Appeal upholds bribery conviction
items.summary: A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Noorin Badaruddin, Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin and Datuk Meor Hashimi Abdul Hamid unanimously dismissed Mohd Ridhuan Kasah’s appeal against his conviction and sentence on the second charge. However, Mohd Ridhuan will serve 14 months’ imprisonment as both jail terms are to run concurrently. The court ordered Mohd Ridhuan to begin serving his sentence immediately.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia must move beyond conventional security methods to tackle AI, drone threats
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia must shift from conventional security methods to a more comprehensive strategy to address rapidly evolving threats, including emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), post-quantum cryptography and drones, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the changing security landscape required close collaboration between the government, private sector and the public to ensure the country’s security remained safeguarded. “We must foster synergy through a coordinated approach involving government departments and ministries,” he said when launching National Security Month 2026, organised by the National Security Council (MKN), here today.

items.title: Ringgit closes flat as FOMC minutes weigh on US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed flat against the US dollar after the latest US Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes suggested a less unified stance on further policy tightening, an analyst said. At 6pm, the ringgit was traded at 4.0750/0795 against the US dollar from 4.0750/0800 at Wednesday's close. Currently, the US federal fund rates stood at between 3.50 per cent and 3.75 per cent.

items.title: Man arrested, drugs worth over RM450,000 seized in Penang
items.summary: NOBONG TEBAL: Penang police have crippled a liquid syabu processing and distribution syndicate with the arrest of a 40-year-old man and the seizure of drugs worth more than RM450,000. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said police also seized about 9kg of liquid syabu, estimated to be worth more than RM450,000, during the operation in Nibong Tebal. Police also seized assets worth about RM70,000 under the Dangerous Drugs (Forfeiture of Property) Act 1988.

items.title: Jaecoo J8: Big SUV at disruptive price point
items.summary: THE Jaecoo J8 enters Malaysia as one of the newer challengers in the crowded D-segment SUV space, but it does so with a clear strategy: offer size, luxury features and technology that punch above its price bracket. It is not trying to reinvent the SUV formula. Instead, it focuses on something more straightforward - giving buyers a large, well-equipped, premium-feeling SUV at a price that undercuts many established Japanese and European rivals.

items.title: BN candidates upbeat despite contesting in PH strongholds in Stulang, Perling
items.summary: Both candidates said they would continue working hard until the final day of campaigning tomorrow. The campaign will continue right until the last day," they said during the Bicara PRN Johor podcast. MCA newcomer Bong said the past two weeks of campaigning had revealed a noticeable shift in voters' reception towards BN.

items.title: Perlis MB expects tourism boost as Kuala Perlis-Satun ferry resumes after Covid-19 hiatus
items.summary: KANGAR, July 9 — Perlis expects tourist arrivals to reach between 5.5 million and six million this year following the resumption of the Kuala Perlis-Satun ferry service today, which is expected to further boost the state’s tourism sector. Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah said the reopening of the sea route, suspended since the Covid-19 pandemic, would facilitate the movement of tourists between Satun and Kuala Perlis while generating economic spillover for local communities and contributing to the state’s economy. “I believe Kuala Perlis will also receive more tourists from Thailand to shop here, which will help increase state revenue while strengthening ties between Malaysia and Thailand, particularly Perlis and Satun,” he told reporters after welcoming a delegation from Satun at the Kuala Perlis Ferry Terminal today.

items.title: Russian frustration rises as fuel crisis bites
items.summary: IN Russia's grain belt, farmers fret they will not be able to harvest their crops as a fuel crisis sparked by Ukraine's drone attacks on oil refineries and depots disrupts daily life. As Kyiv tries to pressure Moscow into making peace with strikes on the country's energy infrastructure, the resulting damage has squeezed oil-rich Russia's fuel supplies, leading to restrictions in most regions and growing public disquiet. This has pushed drivers to crowd-source maps and trade tips about which stations have fuel and shorter lines.

items.title: DPMM welcomes digital foreign worker quota system, calls for continued improvements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Malay Chamber of Commerce Malaysia (DPMM) hopes the digital foreign worker quota application system via the e-Quota module in the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) will be monitored and enhanced to keep the approval process efficient, transparent, and responsive to industry needs. DPMM President Norsyahrin Hamidon said system stability, processing times, procedural clarity, and employer support must be closely monitored to achieve the objectives of foreign worker management reform. “DPMM hopes the implementation of this new system will be constantly monitored and improved to keep the approval process efficient, transparent, and responsive to industry needs,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Govt reviewing maritime laws, mulls tougher penalties for marine pollution offences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The government is reviewing Malaysia’s maritime legal framework, including the possibility of imposing tougher penalties for marine pollution offences, through a comprehensive study by the Malaysia Maritime Law Review and Reform Committee (MLRRC). Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the committee has been tasked with assessing the effectiveness of existing maritime legislation and identifying areas requiring amendments or improvements, particularly in navigational safety, marine environmental protection and ship-sourced pollution. He said the government will continue to review policies, legislation and implementation mechanisms from time to time to ensure the country’s marine pollution management framework remains effective, efficient and in line with developments in the maritime sector and international best practices.

items.title: Murad ‘Baddest Coach’ Zaidi receives DNAA on two charges, calls for due process to prevail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Shah Alam Magistrates' Court today granted businessman, high-performance consultant and transformation specialist Syed Mohammad Murad Syed Naseem, better known as Murad “Baddest Coach” Zaidi, a discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) on two charges involving two complainants. The ruling also comes amid a broader legal dispute involving Murad's company, with the same complainants reportedly named in an ongoing civil suit initiated by the company. Speaking on the court’s decision, Murad said the development was never about personal vindication but about reaffirming the importance of allowing the legal process to run its proper course.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia closes lower amid renewed Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Thursday as renewed geopolitical tensions in Middle East weighed on investor sentiment. At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) fell 5.97 points, or 0.36 per cent, to 1,677.64 from Wednesday's close of 1,683.61. Turnover slipped to 2.64 billion units valued at RM2.19 billion from 2.96 billion units valued at RM2.18 billion on Wednesday.

items.title: Witness says 5 letters prepared for Jana Wibawa project bids
items.summary: Nepturis Sdn Bhd director and senior executive procurement Aliza Abd Malek said she prepared the letters on the instructions of businessman Lian Tian Chuan, better known as TC Lian. Apart from preparing a letter for Nepturis, Aliza said she also prepared letters for Permata Rebana Sdn Bhd, Fastcoll Corporation Sdn Bhd, JM Letrik Sdn Bhd and MZ Hakujaya Sdn Bhd, companies owned by Lian's business associates. Aliza: Yes.

items.title: Heatwave linked to more than 5,000 deaths in Germany, RKI says
items.summary: BERLIN: Germany has recorded an estimated 5,120 heat-related deaths so far this year, most of them in late June when weekly average temperatures far exceeded 20C, the Robert Koch Institute (RKI) for public health said on Thursday. Around 4,270 of the deaths were among people aged 75 and older, the RKI said in a weekly report. The EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service said in a bulletin on Thursday that Western Europe had its hottest June on record with an average of 20.74 degrees.

items.title: Parliament to host inaugural fun run on July 25 to promote healthy lifestyle
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parliament, through the Malaysian Youth Parliament (PBMy) secretariat, will organise its inaugural Larian Cergas Parlimen fun run on July 25 to promote a healthy lifestyle among lawmakers and the public. Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Datuk Dr Johari Abdul said the programme would serve as a platform for members of parliament to champion healthy living, particularly among young people. He said the 5km run would start from the Parliament building, pass several landmarks including Tugu Negara, and finish back at Parliament.

items.title: Travel agent gets 5 years' jail, caning for assaulting student during e-hailing ride
items.summary: TAWAU: A travel agent was sentenced to five years' jail and two strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to physically sexually assaulting a public university student three years ago. Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the sentence after accepting the guilty plea from Mohd Shahremy Abdul Raset, 31. He was charged under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.

items.title: US reiterates backing for Tibetan aspirations to preserve culture
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US State Department said on Wednesday it will continue to call on China to return to dialogue with the Dalai Lama, after a Tibetan man set himself on fire near UN headquarters last week. The Tibetan man died a week ago from severe burns suffered near the United Nations headquarters in New York. Voice of Tibet said he "self-immolated outside the UN headquarters in New York after a live appeal for Tibetan independence and unity."

items.title: Malaysia will only import oil from Russia if acceptable from an international trading basis, minister says
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Malaysia will only import oil from Russia if it is "acceptable from an international trading basis", Second Finance Minister Amir Hamzah Azizan said at the Reuters NEXT Asia conference in Singapore on Thursday. Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim's state visit to Russia and Turkmenistan in June yielded new energy commitments, according to state media Bernama. Russia assured Malaysia supplies of petrol, oil, and gas, while in Turkmenistan, Malaysia's state-owned energy firm Petronas signed several new agreements, including collaborations with state companies Turkmennebit and Hazarnebit, to strengthen its presence in the Caspian Sea and expand its upstream portfolio.

items.title: Witness says Lian instructed letters seeking Jana Wibawa projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Businessman Lian Tian Chuan, better known as TC Lian, instructed his subordinate to prepare letters to then prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin seeking government projects under the Jana Wibawa programme, the High Court heard today. Nepturis Sdn Bhd director and senior executive procurement Aliza Abd Malek said Lian personally decided the titles of the letters and instructed her to prepare applications for the Klang Utara district police headquarters project and the Central Spine Road (CSR) project in 2021. She said major decisions involving Nepturis required Lian's approval.

items.title: Abuse cases handled by One Stop Crisis Centre hit five-year high, 12,906 victims in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The number of abuse and violence cases handled through the One Stop Crisis Centre (OSCC) climbed to 12,906 in 2025, compared with 9,652 cases in 2020. Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Women, Children and Social Development deputy chairman Rodziah Ismail said the sharp increase, which reached a five-year high, reflected the growing scale of violence and abuse cases, proving that victim protection was a wider societal issue. The Ampang member of parliament said female victims remained higher in number, with 10,176 cases in 2025 compared with 2,730 male victims, while sexual abuse cases involved 3,852 women and 406 men.

items.title: Philippines rejects Chinese scholars' claim over its island province near Taiwan
items.summary: MANILA: The defence minister of the Philippines on Thursday rejected as "baseless" and "ludicrous" assertions by Chinese scholars that its northernmost island-chain province belongs to Beijing, calling the claim concerning and worthy of challenge. Chinese state-run news site GDToday reported on July 2 that scholars from institutions including Nanjing University argued at a June 30 symposium that Batanes was a natural extension of Taiwan and therefore belonged to China. The assertions may add a new dimension to long-running tensions between Manila and Beijing, which are already embroiled in multiple disputes over islands and features in the South China Sea.

items.title: Johor polls: Tiram needs solutions, not experiments, says BN candidate
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional's Tiram candidate Senator Datuk Halim Sulaimen has urged voters not to repeat what he described as the "emotion-driven" decision made during the 14th General Election (GE14). The Johor Umno secretary said voters should evaluate candidates based on their track record, ability and commitment to serve the constituency rather than being influenced by temporary sentiments. On economic empowerment, Halim said he aimed to help Tiram residents achieve higher incomes, including through Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes.

items.title: Anutin wishes Anwar luck ahead of Johor polls
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has wished Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim success in the upcoming Johor election this Saturday. The Johor election on Saturday will see 172 candidates contesting for 56 state assembly seats, with both Barisan Nasional (BN) and PH fielding candidates in all contested constituencies. Meanwhile, Anwar described his meeting with Anutin as warm and productive.

items.title: Anwar: Myanmar crisis best resolved through inclusive engagement
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia believes the Myanmar crisis can only be resolved through inclusive engagement with all stakeholders while allowing its people to determine their own future, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Speaking at a joint press conference with Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, Anwar said Malaysia values Thailand's views on the issue, given its position as Myanmar's neighbour and a key regional partner. On bilateral security, Anwar said Malaysia appreciated Thailand's commitment to maintaining peace along the two countries' shared border.

items.title: RM13.34m cannabis haul: Selangor police bust four drug syndicates
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the operations carried out by the KLIA district police headquarters Narcotics Crime Investigation Division in collaboration with Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB) auxiliary police led to the arrest of two foreign men, a local man and two local women aged between 25 and 40. He said the first case on June 29 involved the arrest of a 25-year-old foreign man from South-east Asia after authorities detected two suspicious pieces of luggage at KLIA Terminal 1 containing 60 packages of cannabis buds weighing 33.6 kilogrammes (kg). “On the same day, police also arrested a 35-year-old local woman after discovering 39 packages of cannabis buds weighing 21.1 kg in a suitcase at the terminal,” he said in a press conference at the Selangor police contingent headquarters Officers’ Mess today.

items.title: One Stop Crisis Centre sees abuse, violence cases surge to five-year high at over 12,900 in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The number of abuse and violence cases handled through the One Stop Crisis Centre (OSCC) climbed to 12,906 in 2025, compared with 9,652 cases in 2020, highlighting a worrying trend. “Physical abuse still accounts for the highest number of cases, but the significant rise in sexual abuse cases, from 2,699 in 2020 to 4,258 in 2025, is a worrying trend. The Ampang MP said female victims remained higher in number, with 10,176 cases in 2025 compared with 2,730 male victims, while sexual abuse cases involved 3,852 women and 406 men.

items.title: Going beyond encryption, Malaysia rolls out quantum cryptography sandbox to safeguard digital borders
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia has launched a strategic framework to shield the nation from future cyber threats posed by quantum computing, introducing the National Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Plan and the Malaysia Quantum Cryptography Sandbox. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim noted the urgency of these initiatives, saying that Malaysia must rapidly adapt as cyber threats grow increasingly sophisticated. “We are introducing new concepts such as the National Cryptography Policy and our cryptography action plans.

items.title: Anwar urges whole-of-nation approach as cyber, AI threats reshape Malaysia’s security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for a collective national defence, warning that security can no longer be left solely to the military and enforcement agencies as artificial intelligence (AI) and cyber threats reshape Malaysia’s security landscape. “If every family and every household is not prepared, there is a security risk. The prime minister stressed that national security must evolve from a state-led responsibility into one that extends to every household, student, and institution.

items.title: Sabah doctor shortage: Health Ministry plans 560 permanent medical officer postings from October
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) will appoint 560 new permanent medical officers for Sabah starting this October, in a direct bid to ease the state’s chronic doctor shortage, Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib told Parliament today. Nationwide, the ministry is racing to fill 4,500 permanent medical officer posts in two accelerated phases. “In the first phase, 328 officers offered permanent posts were scheduled to report to Ministry health facilities nationwide from June 29, 2026.

items.title: High Court sets Sept 28 hearing on asset freeze bid in 1MDB’s RM1.41b suit against Rosmah over purchase of luxury goods
items.summary: The hearing will run from June 28 to July 2, July 5 to July 9, and July 19 to July 30, 2027. The lawsuit was filed by 1MDB and the 10 companies, including five of its subsidiaries, namely 1MDB Energy Holdings Limited, 1MDB Energy Limited, 1MDB Energy (Langat) Limited, Global Diversified Investment Company Limited and SRC International Sdn Bhd, on May 9, 2024. The other plaintiffs are Affinity Equity International Partners Limited, Alsen Chance Holdings Limited, Blackrock Commodities (Global) Limited, Blackstone Asia Real Estate Partners Limited and Brightstone Jewellery Limited.

items.title: Johor polls: Pakatan rep files police report against Onn Hafiz over alleged TVET campaign misconduct
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, July 9 — The representative of the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Machap seat today lodged a police report against caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi over alleged misconduct involving Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) students during the Johor state election campaign. Khiru Nasir Rohani alleged that there had been a coordinated effort to influence students from local TVET institutions to attend politically motivated programmes aimed at garnering support for Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates. Khiru Nasir, who is also Simpang Renggam Amanah deputy division chief, urged the police, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) and the Election Commission (EC) to conduct a thorough investigation to safeguard the integrity of the democratic process.

items.title: Khairy coy on own candidacy, focuses on driving BN’s ‘Blue Wave’ in Johor
items.summary: KULAI, July 9 — Khairy Jamaluddin sais a decisive Barisan Nasional (BN) victory in the Johor state election would catalyse a “blue wave” for the upcoming polls in Negeri Sembilan and Melaka. “The current mood, especially among Malay voters in particular and non-Malay voters, is quite positive towards BN. Khairy, popularly known as KJ, expressed confidence that BN would contest solo in Negeri Sembilan, arguing that a strong showing in Johor would restore voter confidence and encourage a return to the coalition.

items.title: Johor polls: Zahid pledges full delivery of BN manifesto if voters back coalition
items.summary: KLUANG, July 9 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has pledged to ensure that every promise and commitment outlined in the coalition’s manifesto will be fully implemented if it is given the mandate in the Johor state election. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said fulfilling the manifesto is a top priority to ensure the continuity of development and the well-being of the people in the state. “The promises outlined in the BN manifesto will be fully implemented throughout the next term,” he told a press conference after attending an engagement session with Johor Village Development and Security Committees (JPKK) here today.

items.title: MACC opens 81 probe papers over alleged RM9m false claims under Socso’s Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has opened 81 investigation papers involving 143 companies and detained 98 individuals under Ops Daya over alleged irregularities in incentive claims under the Social Security Organisation’s (Perkeso) Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, involving an estimated RM9 million. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said 77 of those detained had been remanded to assist in the investigation, which is being conducted under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009 and involves 320 workers for the 2024–2025 period. He said that as of yesterday, 81 investigation papers had been opened nationwide, with 69 cases involving agents, companies and individuals recommended for prosecution.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Eight states face thunderstorms, heavy rain until 7pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for eight states until 7pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the affected areas are Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah, as well as Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar and Kinta in Perak. Similar weather conditions are also forecast for Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Tanah Merah, Bachok, Machang and Pasir Puteh in Kelantan; Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus and Kuala Terengganu in Terengganu; and Cameron Highlands in Pahang.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand fast-track border projects as both sides eye US$30b trade by 2027
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia and Thailand have agreed to expedite several border connectivity projects, proceed with the development of special border economic zones and facilitate immigration and customs procedures to strengthen bilateral cooperation and achieve a bilateral trade target of US$30 billion (RM122.3 billion) by 2027. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said his bilateral meeting with Thailand’s Prime Minister today was substantive and fruitful, with both leaders discussing efforts to resolve outstanding issues and enhance cooperation across various sectors. “Malaysia and Thailand are important neighbours.

items.title: MACC opens probe into US$13m overseas property allegedly tied to 1MDB funds
items.summary: MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the investigation, codenamed Op Agam, commenced on June 15 to determine whether any offences had been committed under the MACC Act 2009 and the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001. Abd Halim said the agencies include the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), with which the MACC is cooperating to obtain details on the luxury property. On the ownership of the luxury property, Abd Halim said the investigation is still at an early stage, and several matters have yet to be established.

items.title: Malaysia and Thailand end seafood dispute with landmark agriculture MoU
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia and Thailand have resolved outstanding issues related to fisheries market access and agreed to bring into force the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on Agricultural Cooperation within one week. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Anutin witnessed the exchange of the MoU between Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and his Thai counterpart Suriya Juangroongruangkit. Speaking at the joint press conference, Anwar said the agreement reflected both countries’ commitment to strengthening food security and expanding bilateral trade.

items.title: Johor election: How to find your right polling centre this weekend
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Registered voters for the 16th Johor state election have been advised to check their designated polling centres and voting streams ahead of polling day this Saturday. The Election Commission (EC) said voters can do so through the MySPR Semak portal or the MySPR Semak mobile application. “Let’s fulfil our responsibility as voters in the 16th Johor state election,” the EC said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: MACC clears ex-minister Nik Nazmi of suspicious fund transfers in Dara, Amoi, Kelat elephant case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no evidence of any suspicious fund transfers or financial transactions involving former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad over allegations that he received RM53 million in bribes linked to the transfer of three elephants – Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) – to Japan. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said preliminary checks through the Cash Threshold Report (CTR) and Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) confirmed that no suspicious financial transactions involving the former minister or companies linked to him had been detected. “Based on investigations conducted so far, there is no evidence of any fund transfers or suspicious financial transactions involving the former minister or companies associated with him.

items.title: Malaysian Bar’s challenge to Zahid’s Yayasan Akalbudi DNAA postponed to October
items.summary: Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching granted the adjournment after the lead counsel for the Malaysian Bar, Datuk Ambiga Sreenevasan, informed the court that the Federal Court had fixed Oct 8 to hear the applications by Ahmad Zahid and the Attorney-General (AG) for leave to appeal against a decision made by the Court of Appeal on May 7 this year. The court then fixed Oct 12 for case management to update the outcome of the Federal Court proceedings. The Court of Appeal had overturned the High Court’s decision on June 27, 2024, which had earlier rejected the Malaysian Bar’s application for leave to initiate the judicial review.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Police rule out bullying, cite ‘unresolved grievance’ as likely motive
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 9 — Police believe that an unresolved grievance toward an acquaintance led a Form Three student to stab a classmate at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, last Monday. Selangor Police Chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar clarified that initial investigations rule out bullying as a motive, Bernama reported. Speaking at a press conference at the Selangor Contingent Police Headquarters today, Shazeli noted that other contributing factors include severe stress experienced by the 15-year-old suspect.

items.title: Johor polls: Teo Nie Ching urges voters to return home, beware of fake news
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching has urged Johor voters, particularly those living outside the state or overseas, to exercise their democratic right by returning home to vote in the Johor state election this Saturday. I hope voters working in Kuala Lumpur or Singapore will also cherish that right and come out to vote on July 11,” she said. Meanwhile, Kartiyaini said the PH election machinery is stepping up its campaign to encourage thousands of Johor voters working in Singapore to return home to cast their ballots this Saturday.

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar urges voters to give Pakatan a chance to govern state
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has appealed to the people of Johor to give Pakatan Harapan (PH) a chance to serve the state as they prepare to cast their ballots in the 16th Johor state election this Saturday. The PH chairman said the coalition was committed to working hard to safeguard Johor’s interests if given the mandate by the people. “ Laksa Johor tiada tandingan, Warisan nenda dikenang selalu, Ayuhlah pulang membawa harapan, Makmurlah negeri bertambah maju .” The 16th Johor state election sees 172 candidates vying for 56 state seats.

items.title: Norway defence freeze justified after missile licence cancellation, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 9 — The government’s decision to halt all future procurement and contracts with Norway is justified as the country has failed to show respect for Malaysia, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said the Cabinet had endorsed the Ministry of Defence’s recommendation to stop procuring defence assets from Norway after the country revoked the export licence for a missile system ordered by Malaysia. “Once existing contracts involving Norway expire, the government may no longer engage Norwegian companies or renew those contracts.

items.title: Johor polls: Police approve over 4,000 campaign permits, probe 22 election offence cases
items.summary: Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said between July 7 and 8 alone, police received 884 permit applications, of which 838 were approved after meeting the stipulated requirements. Ab Rahaman said between July 7 and 8, police also received 17 reports and opened four investigation papers into election offences. Cumulatively, Ab Rahaman said police received 73 reports and opened 22 investigation papers related to election offences between June 27 and July 8.
